summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'CI for β₁'?

"CI for β₁" means: b₁ ± t*·SE(b₁) with df = n - 2; a range of plausible values for the true population slope. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Interpretation of slope CI'?

"Interpretation of slope CI" means: We are C% confident that for each one-unit increase in x, the mean response y changes by between [lower, upper] units. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Degrees of freedom (regression)'?

"Degrees of freedom (regression)" means: df = n - 2 for simple linear regression; accounts for estimating both the intercept and slope. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Zero in the interval'?

"Zero in the interval" means: If 0 is in the CI for β₁, we cannot conclude that x and y have a significant linear relationship at that confidence level. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Reading regression output'?

"Reading regression output" means: Standard computer output provides coefficients (b₀, b₁), standard errors, t-statistics, p-values, and R² for interpretation. The other options describe different concepts.
